qualifying_org_definition: Indiana does not provide a self-initiated general nonprofit-volunteer lane for SNAP ABAWD compliance. Per the FSSA/DFR SNAP & TANF Policy Manual Chapter 2400, Section 2438.17.10 (COMPLIANCE WITH ABAWD), there are exactly four compliance paths: (1) working an average of 20 hrs/week = 80 hrs/month (incl. self-employment); (2) an approved SNAP E&T activity through IMPACT, WIOA, or the Trade Act (TAA), 'or any other approved employment and training activity' for >=20 hrs/week (may not be averaged); (3) a combination of work and approved E&T for >=20 hrs/week; (4) a Workfare/CWEP activity for the hours given by the allotment-over-minimum-wage formula. The only nonprofit-adjacent SNAP lane is CWEP (Section 2540.10.30): SNAP recipients 'can perform work in a public service capacity, limited to public and private non-profit employers,' but ONLY at sites that IMPACT establishes via a formal worksite agreement (CWEP/WE Job Request forms), with daily on-site supervision and weekly provider progress reports -- it is administratively assigned, not self-initiated. 'Community Service Programs' (Section 2540.10.40) is explicitly marked (C) = TANF/Cash-assistance only ('TANF recipients perform work...'), is limited to VISTA, AmeriCorps, and State programs, and requires IMPACT approval plus a Community Service Agreement and daily supervision -- it is not a SNAP path. Section 2438.15.05 further states 'Individuals engaged in hobbies or volunteer work (except VISTA or AmeriCorps)... cannot be considered gainfully employed,' confirming Indiana does not treat self-initiated volunteering as qualifying.
qualifyingOrgShort: No self-service nonprofit-volunteer lane exists; the only SNAP nonprofit path is IMPACT-assigned CWEP at a public or private non-profit worksite that has executed a formal worksite agreement with the local IMPACT office and provides daily supervision.
verification_instrument: No Indiana-specific, recipient-submitted volunteer-hours verification form was confirmed from primary sources. ABAWD compliance is documented through the IMPACT system: for E&T/CWEP, the host organization (provider) is 'required to report no less than weekly on the progress/status of each participant' to the IMPACT case manager (Chapter 2500), who records participation in the IMPACT System of Record (SOR); for paid work, employer documentation. Per Section 2438.17.10, 'An individual must verify having met at least one of the above noted conditions prior to becoming eligible for SNAP benefits,' but the manual names no standalone client volunteer form. NOTE: the prior research file cited 'RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE (State Form 44720)' -- this could NOT be corroborated via web search this pass and is therefore demoted to 'unknown / unverified'; do not rely on that form number.
reportingCadence: Monthly at minimum -- providers/host organizations report participant hours to the IMPACT case manager no less than weekly (Chapter 2500); ABAWD status is captured per month in the eligibility system. Simplified reporting applies (Section 2438.17.30) with one exception: if work hours drop below 20 hrs/week, the ABAWD must report the change.
requiredPrestep: For the only nonprofit (CWEP) lane: IMPACT must establish the worksite, execute a CWEP/WE Job Request worksite agreement, and assign the participant; the recipient cannot self-initiate countable nonprofit volunteering. For the general 80-hour lane, the qualifying activity is paid/in-kind work or IMPACT/WIOA/TAA-approved E&T; self-initiated unpaid volunteering is not a recognized path. Participation in IMPACT itself is voluntary for SNAP, but having nonprofit work count requires IMPACT/DFR setup and oversight.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Indiana state policy is silent on the geographic location or incorporation state of a 501(c)(3). Because there is no self-service nonprofit-volunteer lane, the question only arises for CWEP, where the worksite must execute a formal worksite agreement with the local IMPACT office and be subject to daily on-site IMPACT-coordinated supervision -- a de facto in-state/operational requirement. No explicit rule bars or permits an out-of-state nonprofit from holding a CWEP worksite agreement.
hours_formula_detail: The canonical Indiana ABAWD threshold is the federal 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week) for work, paid/in-kind work, and approved E&T (Sections 2438.17.00 and 2438.17.10). The allotment-over-minimum-wage formula applies ONLY to the separate Workfare/CWEP lane, and CWEP is IMPACT-assigned at registered worksites (not a self-service formula lane any recipient can trigger at any nonprofit). Indiana's CWEP formula uses the FEDERAL minimum wage of $7.25 (Indiana's state minimum wage equals the federal $7.25 in 2026), per the manual's own worked example: '$192 allotment divided by federal minimum wage of $7.25 would meet the monthly requirement by having 26 hours in a CWEP activity.' Because Indiana offers no self-initiated general-nonprofit formula lane and no any-nonprofit 80-hour volunteer fallback, the classification is federal_80 rather than workfare_formula or workfare_if_registered; the CWEP registered-site formula is documented here for completeness.
formulaDetail: CWEP/Workfare lane only (IMPACT-assigned, registered worksite): required monthly hours = floor(monthly SNAP allotment / $7.25). Example from the manual: $192 / $7.25 = 26 hours. This is NOT a self-service lane available at any nonprofit; it requires IMPACT to set up the worksite and assign the participant. The general ABAWD requirement is a flat 80 hrs/month of work or approved E&T.
geographic_waivers: None. Indiana's own policy manual (Section 2438.17.05) states regarding waivered labor surplus areas: 'There are currently no such designations.' Indiana has no statewide or county-level ABAWD waiver and historically has not held one. OBBBA/HR1 (signed 7/4/2025) eliminated the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment standard waiver authority (remaining authority limited to areas with unemployment >=10%, or 150% of the national average in AK/HI); USDA moved to terminate existing time-limit waivers around Nov 2, 2025. No FY2026 Indiana ABAWD waiver is on file -- enforced statewide.
geographicWaivers: None in FY2026; ABAWD enforced statewide. Indiana policy manual Section 2438.17.05 states 'There are currently no such designations' for FNS-waivered labor surplus areas. OBBBA removed the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment waiver authority; no Indiana waiver on file for FY2026.
submissionMechanism: Via the IMPACT program: the host organization/provider reports participant hours/progress to the IMPACT case manager (no less than weekly per Chapter 2500), who records them in the IMPACT System of Record (SOR). Employment is documented via employer records. There is no online recipient self-service portal for ABAWD volunteer-hour submission identified in published policy; submission is mediated by the IMPACT case manager / local DFR office.
submission_deadline: No specific calendar-day cutoff is published for ABAWD hour documentation. Providers report participation to the IMPACT case manager at least weekly; ABAWD status is captured per benefit month. Under simplified reporting (Section 2438.17.30), an ABAWD must affirmatively report when work hours fall below 20 hrs/week.
submissionDeadline: No fixed monthly calendar deadline published; provider reports to IMPACT at least weekly, status captured per benefit month. ABAWD must report if hours drop below 20/week.
notes:
FY2026 VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-downloaded and fully extracted both Indiana policy manual chapters (2400, 85 pp; 2500, 42 pp) from in.gov via pymupdf -- both read cleanly. Primary-source confirmations:

(1) Section 2438.17.00 / 2438.17.10: exactly four ABAWD compliance paths -- (a) working avg 20 hrs/week = 80 hrs/month (incl. self-employment); (b) approved SNAP E&T through IMPACT/WIOA/TAA 'or any other approved employment and training activity' for >=20 hrs/week (may not be averaged); (c) combination of work + approved E&T; (d) Workfare/CWEP for the allotment-over-minimum-wage hours. No self-initiated general-nonprofit volunteer lane is listed or implied.

(2) CWEP formula uses the FEDERAL minimum wage of $7.25. Exact manual text: 'The number of hours required to fulfill the requirement with a CWEP activity is calculated by dividing the individual's allotment by the federal minimum wage (rounded down) for example: ABAWD with $192 allotment divided by federal minimum wage of $7.25 would meet the monthly requirement by having 26 hours in a CWEP activity.' Indiana's state minimum wage equals the federal $7.25 in 2026 (unchanged since 2009), so minimumWageUsd = 7.25.

(3) CWEP (Section 2540.10.30) is the ONLY SNAP nonprofit lane: 'SNAP recipients can perform work in a public service capacity, limited to public and private non-profit employers.' But it requires IMPACT to establish the site, a formal worksite agreement (CWEP/WE Job Request forms), daily on-site supervision, and the provider to report at least weekly to the IMPACT case manager. It is administratively assigned, NOT self-initiated volunteering.

(4) 'Community Service Programs' (Section 2540.10.40) is explicitly marked (C) = TANF/Cash assistance ('Community Service Programs are structured programs in which TANF recipients perform work...'), limited to 'VISTA, AmeriCorps, and State programs,' and self-initiated community service 'must be approved by IMPACT Office staff' under a Community Service Agreement with daily supervision. Confirmed excluded from the SNAP ABAWD path.

(5) Section 2438.15.05: 'Individuals engaged in hobbies or volunteer work (except VISTA or AmeriCorps), or any other activity which does not generate sufficient income, cannot be considered gainfully employed and may not be exempt from Work Registration...' -- confirms Indiana's posture that self-initiated volunteering is non-qualifying.

(6) OBBBA/HR1 is reflected in the current manual: age range now 18-64 with the upper exception at 65+, dependent-care exception limited to a child under 14, and new exceptions for Indian/Urban Indian/California Indian (IHCIA) -- all '(as of 7/4/2025).' This confirms FY2026 OBBBA enforcement.

(7) Geographic waivers: Section 2438.17.05 -- 'There are currently no such designations' (FNS-waivered labor surplus areas). No Indiana FY2026 waiver on file; enforced statewide. The current 36-month time period runs July 1, 2024 - June 30, 2027.

(8) Verification: Section 2438.17.10 -- 'An individual must verify having met at least one of the above noted conditions prior to becoming eligible for SNAP benefits.' Chapter 2500 -- 'Any organization providing services to E&T participants is required to report no less than weekly on the progress/status of each participant.' No standalone, recipient-submitted volunteer-hours form is named in the manual. The prior file's 'State Form 44720 / RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE' could NOT be corroborated via web search this pass; verificationFormName demoted to 'unknown'.

CLASSIFICATION RATIONALE: Among the three schema options -- workfare_formula (any nonprofit, self-initiated formula lane), workfare_if_registered (formula only at registered workfare sites, ELSE general nonprofit volunteering = 80 hrs/month), and federal_80 (flat 80 hrs/month, no community-service formula) -- Indiana is classified federal_80. The allotment/min-wage formula exists ONLY in the IMPACT-assigned CWEP lane (registered worksites), which superficially resembles workfare_if_registered; however, Indiana does NOT provide the workfare_if_registered fallback of 'general nonprofit volunteering = 80 hrs/month' -- self-initiated nonprofit volunteering is not a recognized path at all. The dominant, recipient-facing requirement is the flat 80 hrs/month of work or approved E&T. Hence federal_80, with the CWEP registered-site formula documented in formulaDetail. (This is a genuinely close call between federal_80 and workfare_if_registered; see conflictsWithPrior and unresolved.)

TIER: reinstated 3_blocked_or_unclear -- there is no documented self-service path for a recipient to volunteer at an arbitrary nonprofit and have hours count; the nonprofit path (CWEP) requires IMPACT administrative setup, and verification is mediated by the case manager with no recipient-submitted form. For Tended's purposes, Indiana is a state where self-initiated nonprofit volunteer hours likely do NOT count toward ABAWD compliance without IMPACT involvement.

CONFIDENCE: high on classification (federal_80, no self-service volunteer lane), hours (80/month; CWEP = allotment/$7.25), minimum wage ($7.25), and waivers (none, statewide). Medium-only on the verificationFormName (unknown) and on the precise CWEP-vs-general boundary for out-of-state nonprofits (silent).
conflictsWithPrior: verificationFormName: prior file asserted 'RECORD OF IMPACT ATTENDANCE (State Form 44720)' with specific submission cadence; this pass could NOT corroborate that form number via web search and the policy manual names no standalone recipient volunteer form -> demoted to 'unknown'. Do not rely on Form 44720.

minimumWageUsd: prior file did not carry a numeric minimum wage field (formula treated as federal_80 only); this pass records 7.25 explicitly, matching the manual's CWEP example (federal minimum wage $7.25; Indiana state minimum wage also $7.25 in 2026).

classification field name: prior file used hours_formula='federal_80' but did not carry the normalized 'classification' field; added classification='federal_80'. Noted that the choice between federal_80 and workfare_if_registered is close (see unresolved) -- CWEP supplies a registered-site formula but Indiana lacks the general-nonprofit 80-hour fallback that defines workfare_if_registered.

remoteStance normalization: prior 'restricted' -> normalized to 'disallowed' for the new schema, because CWEP/WE require daily in-person worksite supervision and there is no remote-volunteering allowance in Indiana SNAP policy.
unresolved: verificationFormName is unknown: Indiana's policy manual names no standalone recipient-submitted volunteer/community-service verification form for SNAP ABAWD; the prior file's 'State Form 44720' could not be corroborated and was demoted.

Classification boundary: Indiana sits between federal_80 and workfare_if_registered. It HAS an allotment/min-wage formula but ONLY in the IMPACT-assigned CWEP (registered-worksite) lane, and it LACKS the 'otherwise general nonprofit volunteering = 80 hrs/month' fallback that defines workfare_if_registered. Classified federal_80 because no self-service nonprofit lane exists at any hour count; flagged for reviewer judgment.
Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance for a CWEP worksite agreement is not addressed in policy (silent); the daily-supervision/worksite-agreement structure is a de facto in-state operational constraint.

FNS FY2025-2029 waiver page is JS-rendered and could not be statically parsed; Indiana 'no waiver' status is confirmed instead by the Indiana policy manual's own statement ('There are currently no such designations') plus OBBBA waiver-termination context.

Whether DFR will, in practice, accept third-party-documented self-initiated nonprofit volunteer hours toward the 80-hour 'work' path despite the absence of a published self-service lane is not resolvable from published sources -- operationally treated as not a recognized path.
